items.quote: Rachael's intention is to issue an RFP towards end of 2026.

metrics.narrative: Rachael Coad quantified scale but not yet Zycus-specific ROI: indirect spend of roughly £650M/year and direct spend of roughly £2B, with about 30% of indirect spend as tail and 2,000 live suppliers of which 80% sit in unmanaged tail (discovery call, 10 Apr 2026). She also referenced an £80M post-acquisition synergy target but said 'I don't have the answer to that to hand' when asked how much procurement is expected to contribute (10 Apr 2026). Zycus has twice committed to send a quantified savings/business-case model (10 Apr 2026, reiterated after the 15 May 2026 demo) but no confirmation of delivery is on record.

champion.narrative: Rachael Coad is functioning as champion: she said 'I will be running the project, and... it looks like at the moment I will be owning the solution... I'm deeply invested' (10 Apr 2026), personally requested and shaped the demo, and showed 'strong positive engagement with the Zycus platform and Merlin AI capabilities' at the on-site session on 15 May 2026 (Next Step). Her advocacy is real but not yet proven against power: she has no confirmed economic-buyer access, and on 15 May 2026 she raised her own team's lack of headcount/bandwidth to run an implementation programme, a concern that could weaken her ability to carry the deal through to an RFP if unaddressed.

competition.narrative: Greencore already runs several point tools that overlap Zycus's suite: Scanmarket for eProcurement, DocuSign for contract repository ('doesn't tie into anything'), Click for spend visibility ('works well for directs, not well for indirects'), Pactum for AI-driven autonomous negotiation (in its second year of use, mentioned favourably and 'at every possible opportunity'), and an unnamed AI contract-redlining tool acquired roughly three months before the 10 Apr 2026 call that Rachael called 'the best thing ever.' No full-suite S2P competitor has been named; Rachael expects the full competitive field to form only once the RFP launches towards end of 2026.

identify_pain.narrative: Rachael Coad articulated detailed, confirmed pain on the 10 Apr 2026 discovery call: Greencore runs about 7 disparate ERP systems (13 manufacturing sites on Infor System 21, 3 more on separate one-off ERPs, plus a 5-year SAP S/4HANA migration under way); contracts sit in an unmanaged SharePoint folder with a history of accidental auto-renewals; procurement has 'no involvement' in purchase orders under a no-PO-no-pay policy, illustrated by an anecdote of a site buying engineering resource from its nuts-and-bolts supplier; and 80% of 2,000 live suppliers sit in untracked, unmanaged tail spend. She summarized it as 'a lot of what we do is firefighting.'

paper_process.narrative: No paper process exists yet. This is a pre-RFP, pre-business-case opportunity; contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, order form) have not been discussed and would only begin after a vendor-selection outcome that is not expected before an RFP forms towards end of 2026.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is named or engaged. Rachael Coad referenced only 'myself and my boss' as aligned internally and described 'conditioning conversations with the powers that be' as informally supportive (10 Apr 2026), but no name, title, or direct engagement is on record. Budget has not been approved; Rachael must build the business case and get it in front of unnamed senior leadership before an RFP can launch.

decision_process.narrative: The stated sequence is: Rachael Coad builds the business case internally, secures sign-off from unnamed senior leadership, then runs a formal RFP 'towards end of 2026' (10 Apr 2026; Next Step). Rachael herself said she does not yet know the timeline from workshop to go-to-market ('I don't know the answer to that yet,' 10 Apr 2026), so the process is directionally known but not dated with confidence.

decision_criteria.narrative: Rachael Coad asked for a demo tailored to her specific use cases: AI capabilities (autonomous negotiation, savings-opportunity/tail-spend consolidation), contract management, and supplier risk management (10 Apr 2026), and reacted strongly positively to the savings-opportunity agent specifically ('That's really good'). No formal, documented evaluation criteria exist yet because the RFP has not launched; criteria will firm up only once the RFP forms towards end of 2026.

engagement.top_event: Three explicit deliverables (UK food references, ECC6 integration plan, Merlin ROI model) unaddressed and due before BRD fit session on 17 June. These are table stakes for Vendor Selected gate.

competitors.how_we_win: Lean into the buyer's own frustration that Scanmarket 'doesn't tie into anything' as the core case for a single integrated platform.

competitors.name: DocuSign
competitors.quote: We're using DocuSign as our repository. DocuSign doesn't tie into anything.
competitors.how_we_win: Position iContract as replacing an unintegrated repository the buyer already dislikes.

competitors.name: Click
competitors.quote: From a spend visibility, we use Click. Click works well for directs, not well for indirects.
competitors.how_we_win: Target the explicit gap the buyer named (indirect spend visibility) rather than positioning against Click's direct-side strength.

competitors.name: Pactum
competitors.quote: Open to new technology... would we have an AI autonomous negotiation tool? I'd have laughed in your face, and we're in our second year of having it now... Pactum.
competitors.how_we_win: Position Merlin's autonomous negotiation as part of one integrated data core rather than a standalone point tool, and quantify the value of consolidating tail-spend negotiation inside the full S2P platform.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Genuine early-stage promise (an enthusiastic, self-appointed champion and a rich, confirmed pain set) sits alongside real structural gaps: no economic buyer, no approved budget, a stalled internal blocker on implementation resourcing, and a close date that has already lapsed against the buyer's own stated 'end of 2026' RFP timeline.

deal_risk.text: Resourcing: Rachael Coad said on 15 May 2026 that she has no internal headcount/bandwidth to run a procurement transformation programme alongside Greencore's concurrent MBE transformation projects — the stated primary blocker to progression.
Access to power: no economic buyer is identified or engaged; the business case still needs internal sign-off before an RFP can launch (10 Apr 2026).
Forecast: the 12 Apr 2026 close date is already 88 days past due against a buyer-stated 'end of 2026' RFP launch, so the deal will not close on the date currently recorded.

Competitive exposure: Greencore already runs a favoured AI negotiation tool and a newly acquired, well-liked contract-redlining tool, either of which could narrow Zycus's full-suite differentiation if not addressed before the RFP forms.

win_position.text: Rachael Coad described herself as 'deeply invested' and said she will own the solution and run the project (discovery call, 10 Apr 2026), and the on-site demo on 15 May 2026 produced 'strong positive engagement... with Merlin AI capabilities' per the Next Step.

Greencore is evaluating against a genuine, quantified pain set: roughly 7 disparate ERP systems, an unmanaged SharePoint contract repository, no procurement visibility into purchase orders, and 80% of 2,000 suppliers sitting in untracked tail spend (10 Apr 2026).
No economic buyer is named or engaged; Rachael described only an unnamed boss and 'the powers that be' as informally supportive, and budget has not been approved (10 Apr 2026).
The buyer's own timeline defers a formal RFP to 'towards end of 2026' (Next Step), so the recorded 12 Apr 2026 close date is 88 days in the past and is not a credible forecast horizon.

deal_momentum.text: A confirmed two-way buyer email reply was logged on 2 Jun 2026 (about five weeks ago), and Salesforce shows further account activity on 23 Jun 2026 (about two weeks ago), keeping the deal live rather than dark.

Since the on-site demo on 15 May 2026 (about eight weeks ago), no further buyer meeting or call is on record; the only dated forward plan in the Next Step is an EY event invitation for October 2026, several months out.

customer_commitment.text: Rachael Coad has personally driven the evaluation since the first discovery call on 8 Apr 2026, confirmed she 'will be owning the solution,' and requested (and received) a demo tailored to her own use cases on 15 May 2026.
Commitment is still single/double-threaded on Rachael Coad and James Dean; no economic buyer or budget approver has engaged directly.

hard.ais_why:
• The SF Opportunity Description records Greencore's strategic context: 'The organization is currently undergoing a strategic transformation program called Making Business Easier (MBE), aimed at modernizing its supply chain, improving operational efficiency, and enabling data-driven decision-making across the enterprise.' Following the Bakkavor acquisition reaching ~¬£4B revenue, Greencore has 'significantly expanded its scale... increased operational complexity and accelerated the need for digital transformation.' Full Zycus AI suite in scope (ANA, Merlin, Merlin Intake) aligned to this MBE program.
• Qualified-stage with recurring April 2026 working sessions and a scheduled 15 May 2026 meeting attended by Greencore stakeholders James Dean and Rachael Coad with Zycus team (Anthony Gray, Shriya Iyer, Parama Sarkar). BD_Engagement is Unengaged in SFDC but the multi-party meeting cadence suggests active discovery underway. The data-driven decision-making framing from the Description aligns naturally with Merlin Insights and ANA positioning.
• Confidence is Medium because the SF Description provides a strong strategic context but the Avoma summary for this opp returned content from an unrelated account (Avoma fuzzy-match error), so we lack verbatim customer-side AI quotes. The score relies on the Description's explicit transformation language and the agentic/Merlin product mix. A confirmed AI champion or RFP issuance would move this to 8+.

Rachel showed strong positive engagement with the Zycus platform and Merlin AI capabilities. James has confirmed attendance at the Zycus procurement event next week — good opportunity to deepen the relationship in a peer environment. Rachel is impressed with what Zycus can deliver but has raised a significant concern around internal resource availability to support a procurement transformation programme. She does not currently have the internal headcount or bandwidth to run an implementation project. This is the primary blocker to near-term progression.Rachel's intention is to issue an RFP towards end of 2026. Zycus is being positioned to help her build the business case and shape the RFP — giving us a strong influence position ahead of formal process launch.
